steps.title: Identify which gene drives A. sicerae survival
steps.content: Type A flies (with DptA silenced but DptB active) maintain ~90% survival against A. sicerae, while Type B flies (with DptB silenced but DptA active) plummet to nearly 0%. The decisive clue is that survival correlates with the presence of DptB, not DptA.

steps.title: Determine what the differential survival suggests
steps.content: Since DptB alone confers robust defense against A. sicerae, the gene likely evolved specifically for protection against this bacterium—particularly given that DptB does not help against P. rettgeri (stated in the passage). The other choices either reverse which gene provides defense, make unsupported claims about multi-species protection, or assume data not shown. That matches choice C — C is correct.
